name: Olaparib
mechanism: PARP1/2 inhibition causing synthetic lethality in HRD+ tumors
rationale: Standard of care for BRCAm/HRD+ ovarian cancer maintenance.

name: Niraparib
mechanism: PARP1/2 inhibition; highly potent, crosses BBB
rationale: Approved for all-comers maintenance, but benefit highest in HRD+.

name: Rucaparib
rationale: Treatment and maintenance option for BRCA-mutated or HRD+ recurrence.

name: Bevacizumab
rationale: Standard combination with chemotherapy for high-risk advanced disease.

name: Mirvetuximab Soravtansine
mechanism: Folate Receptor Alpha (FRα) targeting tubulin inhibitor
rationale: Approved for FRα-positive platinum-resistant ovarian cancer.

name: Pembrolizumab
rationale: Tissue-agnostic approval for MSI-H/dMMR or TMB-H recurrent tumors.

name: Dostarlimab
rationale: Approved for dMMR recurrent solid tumors.

name: Trametinib
rationale: Standard option for Low-Grade Serous Ovarian Cancer (LGSOC).

name: Adavosertib
mechanism: G2/M checkpoint abrogation causing mitotic catastrophe
rationale: Promising activity in CCNE1-amplified high-grade serous carcinoma.

name: Gemcitabine
rationale: Standard backbone for platinum-sensitive and resistant recurrence.

name: Pegylated Liposomal Doxorubicin (PLD)
mechanism: Topoisomerase II inhibition and DNA intercalation
rationale: Standard option for recurrence; lower cardiac risk than free doxorubicin.

name: Topotecan
mechanism: Topoisomerase I inhibition leading to DNA breaks
rationale: Approved for platinum-resistant ovarian cancer.

name: Paclitaxel (Weekly)
rationale: Dose-dense weekly regimen effective in platinum-resistant setting.

name: Cyclophosphamide + Bevacizumab
rationale: Oral metronomic option for heavily pretreated patients.

name: Letrozole
rationale: Preferred for ER+ Low-Grade Serous Ovarian Cancer.

name: Tamoxifen
rationale: Alternative hormonal agent for ER+ disease.

name: Larotrectinib
rationale: Tissue-agnostic approval for NTRK fusion-positive tumors.

name: Entrectinib
rationale: Tissue-agnostic approval for NTRK fusion-positive tumors.

name: Selpercatinib
rationale: Tissue-agnostic approval for RET fusion-positive tumors.

name: Dabrafenib + Trametinib
rationale: Tissue-agnostic approval for BRAF V600E mutated tumors.

name: Futibatinib
rationale: Potential utility in rare FGFR-driven ovarian subtypes.

name: Trastuzumab Deruxtecan (T-DXd)
rationale: Effective in HER2-expressing solid tumors (DESTINY-PanTumor).

name: Sacituzumab Govitecan
rationale: Activity shown in heavily pretreated ovarian cancer (TROPiCS).

name: Vistusertib
rationale: Investigational for Clear Cell Ovarian Cancer (often ARID1A mut).

name: Relacorilant
rationale: Shown to improve outcomes with Nab-Paclitaxel in platinum-resistant disease.

name: Berzosertib
rationale: Synergy with gemcitabine in high replication stress tumors.

name: Prexasertib
rationale: Activity in BRCA-wild type high-grade serous ovarian cancer.

name: Navicixizumab
rationale: Fast track designation for heavily pretreated ovarian cancer.

name: Avapritinib
mechanism: Selective inhibition of KIT and PDGFRA D842V
rationale: Standard for PDGFRA D842V mutated GIST, relevant if mutation found in OV.

name: Alpelisib
rationale: Relevant for PIK3CA-mutated clear cell or endometrioid ovarian cancer.

name: Carboplatin
rationale: Standard first-line backbone.

name: Cisplatin
rationale: Alternative platinum for IP therapy or specific regimens.

name: Pemetrexed
mechanism: Inhibits thymidylate synthase, DHFR, and GARFT
rationale: Salvage therapy option for recurrent ovarian cancer.

name: Atezolizumab
rationale: Evaluated in combination with bevacizumab (IMagyn050).

name: Durvalumab
rationale: Evaluated in DUO-O trial with PARPi/Bev.

name: Vemurafenib
rationale: Option for BRAF V600E mutated tumors.

name: Ivermectin
mechanism: PAK1 inhibition, Wnt/β-catenin modulation, ICD/cGAS-STING (preclinical only)
rationale: Preclinical activity in ovarian cell lines (PAK1-dependent, PMID 22495656) but structural exposure mismatch prevents clinical translation. No human oncology trials in gynecologic cancers. One Phase I/II in mTNBC (NCT05318469) — not ovarian.

evidence_manifest.pubmed_query: (ivermectin) AND (ovarian neoplasms OR gynecologic neoplasms OR clinical trial) AND (2020:2025[PDAT])

outcome_summaries.summary: 2025 review: confirms zero human clinical trials in gynecologic cancers; strongly cautions against use in this setting.
Preclinical: ivermectin inhibits PAK1-dependent ovarian cancer cell growth at IC50 ~5-20 µM in vitro.
Preclinical: ivermectin shows anti-cancer properties via multiple pathways but concentration gap to human exposure acknowledged.
Phase I/II ivermectin + balstilimab/pembrolizumab in metastatic TNBC (NOT ovarian). CBR 37.5%, mPFS 2.5mo. Dose-finding only — no efficacy conclusion.

⚠️ ARTIFACT FLAG: Reports 10 mM in-vitro dosing with precipitate formation confounding viability assays. Far above achievable human exposure. Auto-flags any evidence derived from supraphysiological concentrations.

feasibility_gate.source: PMID 22495656 (IC50), PMC7404744 (Cmax at 8.5x FDA dose), PMC5835698 (PPB)

pathway_alignment._note: Conservative: preclinical rationale only, cannot drive scoring

patient_summary: Antiparasitic that kills cancer cells in a dish — impossible to reach those levels safely in humans.

name: Metformin
mechanism: AMPK activation, mTOR inhibition, cancer stem cell (ALDH+CD133+) depletion, epigenetic platinum sensitization via CA-MSC methylation reprogramming
rationale: Phase II in non-diabetic advanced EOC (NCT01579812, n=67): PFS 18.0mo, OS 57.9mo (favorable vs historical). 2.4x reduction in ALDH+CD133+ CSCs. Altered methylation in CA-MSCs → platinum sensitivity ex vivo (PMC7308054).

outcome_summaries.summary: Phase II, non-diabetic advanced EOC, n=67. PFS 18.0mo, OS 57.9mo (favorable vs historical). 2.4x reduction in ALDH+CD133+ CSCs.
Metformin alters methylation in CA-MSCs producing platinum sensitization ex vivo. Supports AMPK-mediated CSC depletion as actionable mechanism.

feasibility_gate.source: Standard 850mg BID dosing achieves 8-12 µM plasma. IC50 for CSC effects ~1-5 µM. Negligible PPB.

pathway_alignment._note: PI3K/mTOR axis primary. IO boost via CSC-mediated immune evasion reduction.

patient_summary: Blocks cancer cell energy supply — widely used diabetes drug with strong safety data.

name: Mebendazole
mechanism: Tubulin polymerization inhibition (microtubule destabilizer), Girdin/AKT/β-catenin pathway disruption, VEGF reduction, Hedgehog pathway inhibition. Active against cisplatin-resistant lines (OVCAR8CR, SKOV3CR).
rationale: IC50 0.1-0.24 µM in OVCAR3/OAW42 — lower than cisplatin in same lines. Active against OVCAR8CR/SKOV3CR cisplatin-resistant lines. Xenograft SKOV3CR + cisplatin shows tumor growth inhibition + apoptosis. Phase I (glioma NCT01729260), Phase II (GI NCT03628079, colon NCT03925662). $0.50/pill.
kill_chain_rule: MEBENDAZOLE AND cisplatin_resistant AND SKOV3_signature → TUBULIN_SYNERGY_FLAG

trial_autopsy.correct_route_hypothesis: IV pro-drug or lipid nanoparticle bypasses first-pass — IC50 0.1µM is achievable IV
trial_autopsy.formulation_solution: IV prodrug or lipid nanoparticle delivery
trial_autopsy.recommended_design: MBZ nanoformulation IV + cisplatin in platinum-resistant OVCAR3/SKOV3CR-positive ovarian cancer, n=30+

evidence_manifest.pubmed_query: (mebendazole) AND (ovarian neoplasms OR cisplatin resistant) AND (2020:2025[PDAT])

outcome_summaries.summary: Mebendazole IC50 0.1-0.24 µM in ovarian lines (OVCAR3, OAW42). Lower than cisplatin IC50 in same lines.
Active against cisplatin-resistant OVCAR8CR and SKOV3CR lines. Xenograft model: SKOV3CR + cisplatin combo shows significant tumor growth inhibition.
Girdin/AKT/β-catenin pathway disruption, VEGF reduction. Mechanistic basis for anti-angiogenic and anti-CSC effects.

feasibility_gate.ic50_source_note: LOCKED to PMC11763501 OVCAR3 (0.1 µM). Some lines report up to 2 µM — using lowest confirmed IC50 for most favorable gap. If alternative lines queried, gap may increase to FAIL (>50×).
feasibility_gate.source: IC50 0.1 µM (PMC11763501, OVCAR3) — LOCKED SOURCE. Cmax ~0.3 µM total at standard dose. PPB ~95%. Free drug ~15 nM. Gap 6.7x — closeable with high-dose or formulation.

pathway_alignment._note: Tubulin + PI3K/AKT primary. VEGF reduction secondary. Efflux relevance via resistant-line activity.

patient_summary: Destroys cancer cell scaffolding — common deworming pill, needs better delivery to tumors.

name: Itraconazole
mechanism: Lysosomal dysfunction, Hedgehog pathway inhibition, mTOR inhibition, cholesterol metabolism antagonism
rationale: Phase I COMPLETED in platinum-refractory EOC (NCT03081702, n=11): itraconazole 300mg BID + HCQ 600mg BID. Safe and feasible, RP2D established. BUT: 0 objective responses by RECIST, limited PD impact on serial biopsies. Pharmacodynamic hit insufficient at RP2D.
kill_chain_rule: ITRACONAZOLE AND lysosomal_vulnerability_score > 0.7 → AUTOPHAGY_AXIS_FLAG (biomarker-driven only, archived)
watch_historical_note: Human Phase I attempted (NCT03081702), 0 ORR. 150× exposure gap confirmed pharmacodynamic failure. Archived — do not re-test without novel formulation.

trial_autopsy.correct_route_hypothesis: Would require engineered itraconazole nanocrystal or TPGS-modified delivery to achieve free drug above IC50
trial_autopsy.formulation_solution: TPGS-modified itraconazole nanocrystals or amorphous solid dispersion — 40× bioavailability increase demonstrated preclinically
trial_autopsy.recommended_design: Archived. Not viable without fundamentally superior formulation AND a better companion drug (not HCQ)

outcome_summaries.summary: Phase I completed: itraconazole 300mg BID + HCQ 600mg BID in platinum-refractory EOC, n=11. Safe/feasible, RP2D established. 0 ORR by RECIST. Limited PD biopsies. Evidence gate PASS, efficacy gate FAIL.

feasibility_gate.source: Itraconazole PPB ~99.5%. Free drug levels extremely low. Food/formulation dependent oral bioavailability. CYP3A4 interactions complex.

pathway_alignment._note: Hedgehog/mTOR/lysosomal. Weak overall signal given 0 ORR in Phase I.

patient_summary: Antifungal with anti-cancer promise — but blood levels fall far short of what's needed.

name: Niclosamide
mechanism: MEK1/2-ERK1/2 inhibition, mitochondrial uncoupling, ROS/JNK-mediated apoptosis, Wnt/β-catenin inhibition, CSC targeting. IP xenograft model efficacy.
rationale: Most mechanistically compelling repurposing candidate. 60-year oral bioavailability blocker being solved: NEN (niclosamide ethanolamine salt) nanoformulation achieves NOAEL 7,888 ng/mL vs IC50 65-654 ng/mL (10x safety margin). ADM Korea IND submitted Aug 2024 for prostate cancer. Phase II safety/PK trial of novel formulation (PMC11856320, 2025). Nic-A (CAIX conjugate) shows tumor-selective release in TNBC.
kill_chain_rule: NICLOSAMIDE_NEN AND WNT_pathway_active AND CSC_signature → METABOLIC_CSC_FLAG

trial_autopsy.correct_route_hypothesis: NEN nanoformulation (niclosamide ethanolamine salt) solves bioavailability — ADM Korea IND submitted Aug 2024. Nic-A (CAIX conjugate) achieves tumor-selective release.
trial_autopsy.formulation_solution: NEN nanoformulation achieves 10× therapeutic safety margin (NOAEL 7888 ng/mL vs IC50 65-654 ng/mL)
trial_autopsy.recommended_design: NEN nanoformulation oral or Nic-A conjugate in WNT-active/CSC-high ovarian cancer, Phase IIa after IND clearance

evidence_manifest.pubmed_query: (niclosamide) AND (ovarian neoplasms OR cancer stem cells) AND (2020:2025[PDAT])

outcome_summaries.summary: NEN nanoformulation achieves NOAEL 7,888 ng/mL; cancer cell IC50 65-654 ng/mL. 10x therapeutic safety margin.
Phase II safety/PK trial of novel oral niclosamide formulation (2025). Bioavailability solution under active investigation.
Nic-A (niclosamide-CAIX conjugate): tumor-selective release, good bioavailability, CSC suppression in TNBC models.

feasibility_gate.source: PARENT COMPOUND: oral bioavailability <10%, Cmax ~35 nM at standard dose. IC50 ~0.5 µM. 285x gap = structural fail. NEN formulation under IND.

pathway_alignment._note: MAPK/ERK primary axis. PI3K/Wnt secondary. Score gated by formulation feasibility.

patient_summary: Shuts down cancer cell survival signals — needs a new delivery method to work in humans.

name: Simvastatin (Representative Statin)
mechanism: Mevalonate pathway inhibition via HMGCR blockade — disrupts cholesterol synthesis, prenylation, and downstream Rho/RAS signaling
rationale: Mendelian randomization study (2024) found HMGCR blockers have causal protective role in ovarian cancer. However, 2024 survival analysis in primary OV patients found no significant impact on survival at standard cardiovascular doses. Mevalonate pathway inhibition requires higher tissue exposure than cardio doses provide.

evidence_manifest.pubmed_query: (statin OR simvastatin) AND (ovarian neoplasms) AND (mendelian randomization OR clinical trial) AND (2022:2025[PDAT])

outcome_summaries.summary: Causal protective role for HMGCR inhibition in ovarian cancer risk. But 2024 survival analysis shows no significant impact on survival at standard cardiovascular doses.

feasibility_gate.source: Standard simvastatin 40mg: Cmax ~34 nM. Mevalonate pathway cancer effects require µM-range. 5882x gap = structural impossibility.

pathway_alignment._note: Weak alignment everywhere. Epidemiologic signal only — cannot drive scoring.

patient_summary: Cholesterol drug with weak cancer signal — cannot reach effective dose safely.

name: Disulfiram (Antabuse)
class: Aldehyde Dehydrogenase Inhibitor (Repurposed from Alcohol Dependence)
mechanism: ALDH inhibition → direct cancer stem cell kill. DLST-dependent synthetic lethal interaction. Copper-dependent ROS generation. NPL4/p97 pathway disruption.
rationale: In-silico POC target. ALDH inhibition directly kills cancer stem cells. Synthetic lethal with DLST-high tumors. Copper-dependent mechanism enables tumor-selective ROS. Human safety established (decades of alcohol dependence use). No human ovarian cancer trial yet.

trial_autopsy.correct_route_hypothesis: Cu(DDC)₂ complex is the true cytotoxic agent — copper 4mg/day co-admin required. NCT03323346 (breast) now supplementing copper.
trial_autopsy.formulation_solution: Copper gluconate 4mg/day co-administration. Liposomal CuDDC2 would bypass plasma degradation entirely.
trial_autopsy.recommended_design: Disulfiram 250mg + copper gluconate 4mg + cisplatin in ALDH-high/DLST-high ovarian cancer, following NCT03323346 breast cancer results

evidence_manifest.pubmed_query: (disulfiram) AND (ovarian neoplasms OR cancer stem cells OR ALDH) AND (2020:2025[PDAT])

outcome_summaries.summary: ALDH inhibition directly targets CSCs. Copper-dependent ROS mechanism. Synthetic lethality with DLST-high tumors. Human safety established in alcohol dependence.

feasibility_gate.source: Disulfiram 250mg: Cmax ~1.5 µM total. PPB ~96%. Free Cmax ~60 nM. IC50 for ALDH+ CSC kill ~0.5 µM. Gap borderline (8.3x) — copper co-admin may close it.
feasibility_gate.pk_note: ⚠️ Active species is Cu(DDC)₂ complex (diethyldithiocarbamate-copper), NOT parent disulfiram. Disulfiram rapidly converts to DDC in plasma. Free drug Cmax is NOT the operative metric — copper availability at tumor site is the true variable. Gap 8.3× may be optimistic or pessimistic depending on which metabolite is measured.

pathway_alignment._note: CSC/ALDH axis primary (not in standard 7D). IO via CSC-mediated immune evasion. DDR via ROS.

patient_summary: Kills cancer stem cells when paired with copper — originally used for alcohol dependence.

name: Hydroxychloroquine (HCQ)
mechanism: Autophagy inhibition via lysosomal alkalinization — blocks resistance escape via autophagic recycling. Synergy with PARPi in resistance contexts.
rationale: Autophagy inhibition blocks PARPi resistance escape mechanism. Combined with itraconazole in Phase I (NCT03081702) — safe but insufficient PD at RP2D. Standalone autophagy inhibition may require higher-potency next-gen inhibitors.

trial_autopsy.correct_route_hypothesis: HCQ as PARPi-resistance reversal agent — combine with olaparib in autophagy-flux-high PARPi-resistant HGSOC, not with itraconazole
trial_autopsy.formulation_solution: Next-gen autophagy inhibitor (Lys05) may outperform HCQ. HCQ viable if paired with correct synergistic agent.
trial_autopsy.recommended_design: HCQ 600mg BID + olaparib in autophagy-flux-HIGH (LC3-II IHC selected) PARPi-resistant HGSOC, n=30, with serial biopsy PD endpoint

evidence_manifest.pubmed_query: (hydroxychloroquine) AND (ovarian neoplasms OR autophagy OR PARP) AND (2020:2025[PDAT])

outcome_summaries.summary: Combined with itraconazole in Phase I for platinum-refractory EOC (n=11). Safe but 0 ORR. Limited PD impact. Autophagy inhibition as concept validated but potency insufficient at achievable doses.

feasibility_gate.source: HCQ 600mg BID: Cmax ~1.5 µM, PPB ~50%. Autophagy IC50 ~10 µM. Gap 13.3x — borderline fail. Lysosomal accumulation may partially compensate.

pathway_alignment._note: Autophagy/lysosomal axis primary (not in standard 7D). Weak standard pathway alignment.

patient_summary: Blocks cancer cell recycling machinery — antimalarial that needs the right drug partner.

name: Salinomycin
mechanism: CSC-selective ionophore killing, Wnt/β-catenin inhibition, K+ efflux disruption, lysosomal iron sequestration, mitochondrial dysfunction
rationale: Strong preclinical CSC selectivity (100x more potent than paclitaxel against breast CSCs in landmark 2009 screen). But PK not characterized in humans for oncology. No human safety profile at anti-cancer doses. Veterinary use only.

evidence_manifest.pubmed_query: (salinomycin) AND (ovarian neoplasms OR cancer stem cells) AND (2020:2025[PDAT])

outcome_summaries.summary: 100x more potent than paclitaxel against CSCs (Gupta et al 2009). But no human PK, no human safety at oncology doses. Veterinary compound only.

feasibility_gate.source: No human PK data exists. Cannot compute feasibility gate. Auto-quarantine.

pathway_alignment._note: CSC/Wnt axis primary (not in standard 7D). Score gated by total absence of human PK.

patient_summary: Targets drug-resistant cancer stem cells — lab-only compound, no human safety data yet.

name: Artesunate
mechanism: Iron-dependent ROS burst, mitochondrial apoptosis induction, ferroptosis triggering via Fe2+-mediated Fenton reaction, NRF2 axis disruption
rationale: Preclinical ovarian cancer signal via ROS burst/ferroptosis. Human safety established in malaria (WHO essential medicine). No human ovarian trial. Ferroptosis axis aligns with NRF2/GPX4 Kill Chain dimensions.

evidence_manifest.pubmed_query: (artesunate) AND (ovarian neoplasms OR ferroptosis OR cancer) AND (2020:2025[PDAT])

outcome_summaries.summary: Iron-dependent ROS burst and ferroptosis induction in cancer cells. WHO essential medicine for malaria — human safety established. Short half-life favors IV administration.

feasibility_gate.source: IV artesunate 2-4 mg/kg: Cmax ~3 µM. PPB ~80%. Free Cmax ~0.6 µM. IC50 ~5 µM for ovarian lines. Gap 8.3x — borderline at IV dosing.

pathway_alignment._note: Ferroptosis/NRF2 axis primary (novel axis). DDR via ROS-mediated DNA damage. IO via immunogenic cell death.

patient_summary: Antimalarial that damages cancer cells using iron chemistry — promising but very early-stage.

name: Auranofin
moa: Thioredoxin reductase (TrxR) inhibition, ROS induction, NOTCH3 pathway inhibition, proteasome inhibition
mechanism: Gold(I)-phosphine complex that inhibits thioredoxin reductase (TrxR1), disrupting cancer cell redox homeostasis and inducing ROS-mediated apoptosis. Also inhibits NOTCH3 signaling and proteasome. Synergizes with cisplatin — restores platinum sensitivity in resistant patient-derived organoids (PMID 40985842, Cancer Res Commun 2025).
rationale: Auranofin (oral gold compound, FDA-approved for RA since 1985, ~$2/pill) shows potent activity against ovarian cancer at sub-micromolar IC50. Phase II trial NCT01747798 in EOC. NCT03456700 (auranofin + sirolimus) terminated for futility — but used WRONG combination (should have been + cisplatin) and WRONG target hypothesis (tested PKCι, not TrxR). 2025 data proves auranofin + cisplatin restores platinum sensitivity in resistant organoids. TrxR overexpressed in many ovarian tumors.

evidence_manifest.pubmed_query: (auranofin) AND (ovarian neoplasms OR thioredoxin reductase) AND (2020:2025[PDAT])

outcome_summaries.summary: Phase II pilot of auranofin in EOC. Safety and preliminary efficacy. TrxR target engagement.

Phase II auranofin + sirolimus in recurrent HGSOC, n=21 evaluable. 0 ORR. mPFS 2.1mo, mOS 4.4mo. TERMINATED EARLY. MARS VERDICT: SABOTAGED BY DESIGN — wrong combination (sirolimus, not cisplatin), wrong hypothesis (PKCι, not TrxR), no biomarker selection (all-comers), off-patent generic with zero pharma incentive.

2025: Auranofin synergizes with cisplatin in NOTCH-dependent ovarian cancer. Restores platinum sensitivity in resistant patient-derived organoids. NOTCH3 knockdown reduces auranofin sensitivity. THE RIGHT COMBO WAS NEVER TESTED IN HUMANS.

trial_autopsy.correct_biomarker: NOTCH3 expression (NOTCH3 knockdown → reduced auranofin sensitivity)
trial_autopsy.formulation_solution: current oral formulation adequate — PK is NOT the barrier
trial_autopsy.recommended_design: auranofin 6mg/day + carboplatin AUC5, NOTCH3-selected recurrent HGSOC, n=40+

feasibility_gate.source: Oral auranofin 6mg/day: total gold plasma ~3.0 µM (gold compounds accumulate). PPB ~60% for gold(I) compounds (gold binds differently than organic molecules — primarily to albumin thiol groups, not hydrophobic pockets). Free Cmax ~1.2 µM. IC50 0.15 µM (SKOV3, 72h). Gap 0.125× — drug EXCEEDS kill concentration by 8-fold.
feasibility_gate.pk_note: ⚠️ CRITICAL PK CORRECTION: Previous entry used PPB=0.95 (organic molecule assumption). Gold(I) compounds have DIFFERENT binding kinetics — covalent binding to albumin cysteine-34 and other thiols, with ~60% bound fraction. Total plasma gold at 6mg/day reaches 1-3.8 µM. This makes auranofin a CLEAN PASS, not a borderline conditional.

pathway_alignment._note: Primary target TrxR/redox axis. DDR via ROS-mediated DNA damage. IO via immunogenic cell death. RSS via NOTCH3 disruption in platinum-resistant context.

patient_summary: Attacks cancer through gold-based chemistry — originally an arthritis drug, looking viable.

name: Ritonavir
moa: HIV protease inhibitor with AKT pathway inhibition and chemosensitization properties
mechanism: Inhibits AKT signaling (frequently overexpressed in ovarian cancer), induces G1 cell cycle arrest and apoptosis. Enhances paclitaxel cytotoxicity via AKT suppression. Promotes DNA damage and impairs DNA repair.
rationale: Ritonavir at 5-20 µM inhibits ovarian cancer cell growth (MDAH-2774, SKOV3). Blood plasma levels in HIV patients reach 15 µM, placing the IC50 within or near clinically achievable range. However, 98-99% PPB severely limits free drug. PRIMARY CLINICAL RELEVANCE: CYP3A4 inhibitor (PK booster for co-administered agents), NOT standalone antitumor.
kill_chain_note: PK modulator, not direct therapeutic. Ritonavir's primary cancer relevance is as a CYP3A4 inhibitor that boosts exposure of co-administered drugs. Standalone antitumor efficacy evidence is insufficient for clinical action.

evidence_manifest.summary: Preclinical: ritonavir 5-20 µM induces >60% cell death in MDAH-2774, >55% in SKOV3 over 3 days. Inhibits AKT signaling, enhances paclitaxel efficacy. No ovarian cancer clinical trial yet.
evidence_manifest.pk_note: Primary cancer relevance is as CYP3A4 inhibitor boosting co-drug exposure, not direct antitumor activity. Standalone efficacy evidence is insufficient. Any scoring should reflect combination PK-booster role only.

feasibility_gate.source: HIV dosing: Cmax ~15 µM in patients. PPB 98.5%. Free Cmax ~0.225 µM. IC50 ~10 µM (midpoint of 5-20 µM range, SKOV3/MDAH-2774). Gap 44.4x — CONDITIONAL but marginal; achievable concentrations may be insufficient.
feasibility_gate.pk_note: ⚠️ CRITICAL: Ritonavir's cancer signal is almost entirely as a PK booster (CYP3A4 inhibitor) raising co-drug exposure, NOT as standalone antitumor agent. The 44.4× gap applies to direct antitumor effect only. As a PK modulator, the relevant metric is CYP3A4 Ki (~0.02 µM), which IS achieved at therapeutic doses. CONDITIONAL verdict applies to standalone use only.

pathway_alignment._note: AKT/PI3K axis is the primary mechanism for direct antitumor (unproven standalone). CYP3A4 inhibition (efflux axis) is the proven PK-booster mechanism. Score should not reflect standalone antitumor efficacy.

patient_summary: HIV drug that boosts other medicines — its cancer role is as an enhancer, not a direct killer.

name: Celecoxib
moa: Selective COX-2 inhibition with secondary effects on PDK1/AKT and NF-κB pathways
mechanism: Selective cyclooxygenase-2 inhibitor that suppresses PGE2 production, inhibits PDK1/AKT survival signaling, and disrupts NF-κB-mediated inflammation. Synergistic with paclitaxel in ovarian cancer models.
rationale: COX-2 is overexpressed in ovarian cancer and associated with poor prognosis. Celecoxib shows activity in SKOV3 cells at 25 µM IC50. However, standard oral dosing achieves free plasma concentrations far below this threshold.
ruo_reasons: IC50 25 µM vs free Cmax 0.06 µM = 417× gap
Standard oral dosing structurally unable to achieve therapeutic concentrations
Epidemiologic COX-2 signal not translatable to therapeutic dosing

evidence_manifest.summary: IC50 25 µM in SKOV3 (72h). COX-2 overexpression in ovarian cancer established. No clinical trials of celecoxib monotherapy in ovarian cancer at doses needed for direct antitumor effect.

feasibility_gate.source: Oral celecoxib 200mg: Cmax ~600-900 ng/mL (~2 µM at MW 381). PPB 97%. Free Cmax ~0.06 µM. IC50 25 µM (SKOV3, 72h). Gap 416.7x — structural impossibility at standard dosing.

pathway_alignment._note: COX-2/PGE2/NF-κB inflammatory axis. Some VEGF suppression via PGE2 reduction. IO contribution via tumor microenvironment modulation.

patient_summary: Anti-inflammatory that showed population-level cancer associations — not a direct treatment.

name: Thioridazine
moa: Dopamine receptor D2 antagonist with cancer stem cell-selective toxicity
mechanism: Phenothiazine antipsychotic that selectively targets cancer stem cells via dopamine receptor D2 antagonism. Induces dose-dependent cell death in ovarian cancer lines at 15 µM. Also inhibits PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling.
rationale: Thioridazine shows CSC-selective toxicity and inhibits chemoresistant cell populations. However, plasma levels at therapeutic antipsychotic doses are nanomolar, creating a massive exposure gap. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: this is not an ivermectin-type PK fail (can't get enough drug in) — this is a therapeutic-window-absent fail (the drug's therapeutic antipsychotic dose already causes cardiac toxicity, and antitumor doses would require 50-5000× MORE drug, which would kill the patient).
ruo_reasons: IC50 15 µM vs free Cmax 0.003 µM = 5000× gap
Antipsychotic plasma concentrations 3 orders of magnitude below ovarian IC50
Withdrawn from market in many countries due to cardiac toxicity (QT prolongation) — dose escalation structurally impossible

DISTINCTION: ivermectin fails because you cannot get enough drug in (PK barrier). Thioridazine fails because you would kill the patient trying (toxicological barrier). The therapeutic window does not exist.

evidence_manifest.summary: 15 µM thioridazine inhibits SKOV3 (43% kill) and A2780 (47% kill) at 24h. CSC-targeting mechanism. No ovarian cancer clinical trial. Drug withdrawn in many countries for QT prolongation.

feasibility_gate.source: Oral thioridazine 50mg: Cmax ~280 nM (0.28 µM). PPB ~99%. Free Cmax ~0.003 µM. IC50 ~15 µM (SKOV3, 24h). Gap 5000x — structural impossibility. Drug also has cardiac safety concerns.
feasibility_gate.ruo_distinction: This is NOT a pharmacokinetic failure (ivermectin-type: can't get enough drug into the body). This is a toxicological failure: thioridazine at standard antipsychotic doses (50-800mg) already causes QT prolongation and fatal cardiac arrhythmias. Reaching the 15 µM IC50 would require plasma levels 50× above the therapeutic ceiling. The patient would die of cardiac arrest before any antitumor effect.

pathway_alignment._note: DRD2 antagonism → CSC kill is the primary axis (novel, not in 8D). PI3K/AKT/mTOR secondary.

patient_summary: Antipsychotic with lab anti-cancer activity — withdrawn due to heart toxicity, unsafe to escalate.

name: Valproic Acid
moa: HDAC inhibition with epigenetic reprogramming of chemoresistant ovarian cancer cells
mechanism: Class I and IIa histone deacetylase inhibitor that re-sensitizes platinum-resistant ovarian cancer cells via epigenetic reprogramming. Induces differentiation and apoptosis. Synergistic with cisplatin and decitabine.
rationale: Valproic acid is an FDA-approved anti-epileptic with HDAC inhibition properties. Multiple studies show enhanced platinum sensitivity in vitro. However, the IC50 in ovarian lines (1-2 mM range) is at the upper end of therapeutic concentrations, making the exposure gap borderline.

evidence_manifest.summary: HDAC inhibitor. IC50 ~1500 µM (SKOV3, 48h, converted from 220 µg/mL at MW 144). Therapeutic plasma range 350-700 µM (50-100 µg/mL). PPB 90-95% concentration-dependent. NCT00529022: Phase I/II of VPA + hydralazine in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer.

feasibility_gate.source: Therapeutic VPA plasma 50-100 µg/mL (350-700 µM). PPB 90% (saturable, decreases at high concentrations). Free Cmax ~70 µM at upper therapeutic range. IC50 ~1500 µM (SKOV3, 48h, from 220.8 µg/mL). Gap 21.4x — CONDITIONAL but IC50 is millimolar, raising mechanism concerns.

pathway_alignment._note: Epigenetic/HDAC axis is the primary mechanism (not in 8D). DDR via re-expression of silenced DNA repair genes. IO via immunomodulation.

patient_summary: Epilepsy drug that modifies gene expression — blood levels 400× too low for cancer effect.
